item.title: Link to image source.
item.content:encoded:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monarch-butterflies-pacific-grove.jpg

Backup of image source for license verification: archive.org | archive.today

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Oh wow, I can't tell you how flattered I am that you chose to narrate my article, it's great work and awesome in general!. (Sorry this reply is so late, I only log in on the site once in a blue moon these days - usually I just lurk around reading articles without bothering)

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I can't tell you how beautiful I find this skip. I'm so glad this was written and I'm so happy to see the SCP Format used in such a touching way.

Check it out here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so glad that despite made by an artist/musician, this isn't tagged with AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article, +1. Is no one commenting on how hilariously fitting a "Harpo event" is?

item.title: Re: cm
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the plug, Shaggydredlocks! I should note for the record that our conception of C M is not really what Myriad envisaged in this piece. That said, I'm hoping to give our version a run in another On Mount Golgotha tale quite soon.

item.title: Re: cm
item.content:encoded: Yea, actually. They show up in this tale! You also might be interested in this canon as a whole.

item.title: cm
item.content:encoded: Are there any other references to this C M??? Would love to see this character fleshed out. I am an audio engineer so this character intrigues me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I like our shared universe quite a bit, I feel some of the best pieces on the site are those that could work even if you stripped it completely from context. And this is one of those. You could have no idea that the Foundation regularly fights anartists and this would still resonate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That letter, man… ;-;

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems a bit intense in the note but that appears to have been intentional.
+1 all the way for feels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn. Not a lot of things can bring tears to my eyes, but this did the trick. The sheer frustration at the end of that note - poor CM, lost his rival-cum-muse, and her own people can't be assed to give her a proper sendoff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: came expecting keter because of the name, got something much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading this comment is sort of funny because I think the SCP doesn't work without the letter. The letter sells it. I didn't take it as a taunt, more as frustration. (If I'd read it as a taunt, I wouldn't have liked it, because that's yet another cliche.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…ergh.

I honestly didn't want to like this, because it's ostentatiously a Formula SCP: clunky and a bit difficult to wade through, but acceptable tone, and some backstory attached to serve as a point of interest.

But it's a really compelling backstory. Just… damn. It makes the whole thing work; it imbues it all with meaning and emotion. Well played: have another +1 on top of the pile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The technical term is "cremains" (CREmated remAINS, you see), but since it's what's left after the cremation procedure, either "remains" or "ashes" would be appropriate (it's not strictly ash - frequently bits of bone survive the cremation process).
Thank you to projectzerozero in #site19 for pointing this SCP out to me. At first, I thought it'd be a Zyn SCP because butterflies, but it's not. Congratulations, Myriad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just reread this and something bothered me. It states that specialist Smith was cremated and then buried. Plus her remains were later exumed and examined. If she was cremated, wouldn't there just be ash left? Also, were her remains ever re buried, either at the original grave or at the skip site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprisingly high quality and emotionally involving writing. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, there's already a TVTropes listing for this under the SCP Foundation's page. Under "Worthy Foe." This is wonderful and quite beautiful. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, that was sweet.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just loaded a smaller image. Hopefully it solves the problem. Glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A touching tribute to a fallen adversary. UpVoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, well I admit that he is a guy the Foundation can't find, but I really wasn't going for taunting. The letter was supposed highlight his relationship with the Specialist rather than a "take that" to the Foundation. If anything a part of it was so people in the foundation who might actually care wouldn't be left wondering what happened to one of their own.

Still, I'm glad you thought the base effect was interesting even if you didn't like the rest of the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted it, until I got to the letter. To me, it's just another person the Foundation can't find, taunting the Foundation etc etc etc. The idea was pretty interesting without having to add the letter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not going to lie, I'm loving anartist as a term. Works very well imho for the subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad it's not just me! I'm not too fond of 'anartist' because, as you demonstrate, the first thing it parses to is 'anarchist artist.'

Author: Upvote - this was really great. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I saw an early draft of this, but I could be confusing it with something else. Either way, the story behind this is great, and even reading the description, I got the feeling that the surrounding area was somehow a monument to the Specialist. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a love-letter from an anartist to a deceased Foundation operative.

… That is so beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture took an unusually long time to load. But other than that +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Didn't see your post before I commented here. I personally can see where you would assume that portmanteau, but I personally believe "anartist" to just signify "an artist that uses anomalies in his or her artwork" and nothing more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, there is certainly a fringe world of anomalous people beyond the GoIs, certainly (the same way that only a fraction of the people in the world are soldiers, policemen, astronauts or politicians but they get the most movie-time), and I love the idea of an anartist movement that simply tries to create art for art's own sake, without caring about public knowledge but avoiding immediate harm to people.

Also, anomalous music FTW. : I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, yeah, the an- prefix representing anarchist never even occurred to me, though it makes sense in retrospect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anart. Anomalous Art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always sort of assumed that any artist that used anomalous means is more or less defined as an anartist?

I've always interpreted "anartist" as it's used here (mostly in connection with AWCY) as a portmanteau of "anarchist" and "artist", since 99% of the time the anomalous art in question is meant to be harmful and/or purposefully disruptive. Mea culpa if you're operating with it as signifying "anomalous artist", though I don't get the impression that's what the term really means around here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always sort of assumed that any artist that used anomalous means is more or less defined as an anartist?

Though, I suppose you could define Anartist as artists who create art _exclusively_ using anomalous means.

(Or do you just not like the term?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the literal in-universe definition of the word…

EDIT: Couldn't upvote fast enough. Great job, Myriad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's an anartist specializing in music and sound

I really, really wish that every artist using anomalous means in the SCPverse wasn't an "anartist".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoyed it!

CM? Not really - at least not any of the listed ones. He's an anartist specializing in music and sound, but he would absolutely loathe AWCY and what they do. I personally like to think that there's a loose anart community that uses anomalous effects for expression and enhancement of art rather that say, terrorism - if he's a part of anything, he'd be a part of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it. Would CM be part of a GoI?

Upvoted. : )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done, Myriad. The backstory is beautiful, and I love what you wrote in the note. Glad to have been a part of it, and may we see many more stories from you! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't really find any significant faults in this; I really like it. About the only thing I can mention with any negativity is that, at a glance, the image appears to be rather large for the size it is displayed at; this could cause the page to load slowly for some users. This is just a guess, though - it's hard to tell on my tablet's screen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Respect for the fallen foe.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing, and I love the background story of the rivalry that became a passion and him hounouring her like that is a amazing idea. Awsome job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Halfway through, I honestly didn't think i'd like this. Then the negative decibel silence hit and I was intrigued but unsold.

And then that letter. I really like that letter. Not only does it make ALL articles better for the idea of a song between the containment and item descriptions, but it made me care about a character, which is exceptionally hard to do. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job, Myriad. You almost had my upvote after I read it in chat yesterday, but not quite, however with the picture I find it good enough to upvote. I think the concept is especially cool. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image is from Wikimedia Commons
©Agunther/Wikimedia Commons/CC-BY-3.0
(I think I got that right - I hope).

Thanks especially to Zyn and Azzleflux for putting up with my repeated badgering, Smapti for a detailed shredding, and everyone in chat that commented.
